user_request: Draft a suit for recovery of Rs.20,00,000/- paid as advance for a business transaction which failed due to Defendant's default. Plead total failure of consideration under Section 65 of Indian Contract Act. Claim refund with interest and costs. Include cause of action paragraph with accrual date, continuing nature of cause of action, valuation, court fee, and proper verification clause. Draft suitable for filing before the District Court.

facts.summary: Plaintiff seeks recovery of Rs. 20,00,000 paid as advance for a business transaction that failed due to the Defendant's default. The suit claims total failure of consideration under Section 65 of the Indian Contract Act, seeking refund with interest and costs.

slots.value: Recovery of advance with interest and costs

assumptions: Jurisdiction lies with the District Court based on valuation or cause of action
Interest rate to be determined by court or statutory rate

In Ratnavarma Raja v. Vimla, 83 the Supreme Court has observed that, whether proper court fee has been paid 
or not, is a issue between the plaintiff and the state and that, the defendant has no right to question it in a 
revision. In view of this pronouncement, some of the decisions noted above might require reconsideration. 84 
The Allahabad High Court has held that a decision in the trial of a suit as to the amount of court fee, is not an 
independent proceeding and, therefore, not open to revision. 85 The Oudh High Court has held that an order 
demanding deficit court fee from a plaintiff is not open to revision.86

Order 8, r 6A does not bar the filing of a counterclaim even after filing a written statement, provided (i) the cause of 
action had accrued before the defendant had delivered his defence; and (ii) the limitation period has not expired 
under Art. 113 of the Limitation Act 1963. For a money claim, three years is the period of limitation.35
In a suit for declaration of title and possession, the defendant filed written statement. Subsequently, the written 
statement was amended to claim recovery of possession on the ground of trespass alleged to be committed by 
plaintiff. It was held by the Supreme Court that the subsequent filing of counter-claim was not tenable because 
trespass as a cause of act ion for filing counter-claim cannot be said to have arisen prior to filing of written 
statement,36 S.B. Sinha, J., speaking for the Bench, observed as follows:
10. The provision of O VIII, r 6A must be considered having regard to the aforementioned provisions. A right to file 
counter-claim is an additional right. It may be filed in respect of any right or claim, the cause of action therefore, 
however, must accrue either before or after the filing of the suit but before the defendant has raised his defence.

draft_artifacts.title: Plaint under Order XXXVII Rule 1 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908 for Recovery of Rs. 20,00,000/-
draft_artifacts.text:
Plaint under Order XXXVII Rule 1 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908

In the District Court at {{DISTRICT_NAME}}, {{STATE_NAME}}

Plaintiff: {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, (Address: {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}})
Defendant: {{DEFENDANT_NAME}}, (Address: {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}})

STATEMENT OF CLAIM
1. The Plaintiff seeks recovery of Rs. 20,00,000 paid as advance for a business transaction with the Defendant, which failed on account of the Defendant's default. The Plaintiff therefore claims total failure of consideration under Section 65 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872, and prays for refund of the amount together with interest and costs.
2. The payment of Rs. 20,00,000 was made on {{DATE_OF_PAYMENT}} as an advance towards the said business transaction. The Defendant defaulted in performance. The transaction never fructified.

CAUSE OF ACTION
3. The cause of action arises on the date of payment and on the date of failure of the consideration; the right to sue accrued on {{ACCRUAL_DATE}}. The cause of action is continuing in nature as the Defendant has not refunded the amount despite demand, and the money remains due.

PRAYER
4. Relief sought: (i) Refund of Rs. 20,00,000; (ii) Interest on the amount at such rate as this Hon'ble Court may deem just and reasonable from the date of accrual to the date of realization; (iii) Costs of the suit.

VALUATION FOR COURT FEES AND LIMITATION
5. The suit value for purposes of court fee and jurisdiction is Rs. 20,00,000. Court fee payable: {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}} (to be verified at time of filing).

LEGAL BASIS
6. This suit is grounded on total failure of consideration under Section 65 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872. The Pl aintiff pleads Section 65; the underlying facts constitute a total failure of consideration for the advance paid.

LIMITATION
7. The present suit is within limitation under Article 47 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963 — For money paid upon an existing consideration which afterwards fails. Three years. The accrual date is the date of the failure; the present suit is a continuing cause of action.

VERIFICATION
8. Verification: I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, Plaintiff above named, do hereby declare that the contents of this plaint are true and correct to the best of my knowledge and belief. Verified at {{LOCATION}} on this {{FILING_DATE}}.

LIST OF DOCUMENTS / ANNEXURES
Annexure A: Copy of payment receipt; Annexure B: Any correspondence and communications.

Place: {{PLACE}}
Date: {{FILING_DATE}}
Signature of Plaintiff/Authorized Person: ________________________

draft_artifacts.title: Plaint under Section 26 read with Order VII Rule 1 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908 for Recovery of Rs. 20,00,000/- (Advance Paid)
draft_artifacts.text:
PLAINT UNDER SECTION 26 READ WITH ORDER VII RULE 1 OF THE CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE, 1908

IN THE COURT OF THE DISTRICT JUDGE AT {{DISTRICT_NAME}}, {{STATE_NAME}}

Civil Suit No. ____ of 20__

{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}},
S/o/D/o/W/o __________,
Aged about ____ years,
Residing at: {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}}                                             ...Plaintiff

VERSUS

{{DEFENDANT_NAME}},
S/o/D/o/W/o __________,
Aged about ____ years,
Residing at / Carrying on business at: {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}}                   ...Defendant

PLAINT

1. That the Plaintiff and the Defendant entered into a business transaction/arrangement under which the Plaintiff paid a sum of Rs. 20,00,000/- (Rupees Twenty Lakhs only) as an advance on {{DATE_OF_PAYMENT}} towards the said transaction.

2. That the Defendant committed default in performing its obligations in respect of the said transaction, as a result of which the transaction did not fructify and the consideration for which the advance was paid totally failed.

3. That the Plaintiff is entitled to refund/restoration of the said sum of Rs. 20,00,000/- on account of total failure of consideration, and the Plaintiff specifically pleads Section 65 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872.

4. That the Plaintiff has demanded refund of the said advance from the Defendant, but the Defendant has failed and neglected to refund the amount, and continues to wrongfully retain the Plaintiff’s money.

5. The Plaintiff relies upon the following documents:
   a. Proof of payment/receipt evidencing payment of Rs. 20,00,000/- as advance dated {{DATE_OF_PAYMENT}} (Annexure A).
   b. Correspondence/communications exchanged between the parties in relation to the transaction and refund demands (Annexure B).

JURISDICTION
6. This Hon’ble Court has territorial jurisdiction to entertain and try the present suit because {{TERRITORIAL_JURISDICTION_FACTS}}.

7. This Hon’ble Court has pecuniary jurisdiction as the value of the suit for the purposes of jurisdiction and court fees is Rs. 20,00,000/- (Rupees Twenty Lakhs only), which is within the pecuniary limits of this Hon’ble District Court.

CAUSE OF ACTION
8. The cause of action first arose on the date when the consideration failed and the Plaintiff’s right to claim restoration/refund accrued, i.e., on {{ACCRUAL_DATE}}. The cause of action further arose and continues to subsist on subsequent dates when the Defendant failed/refused to refund the amount despite demands, and as the Defendant continues to wrongfully retain the Plaintiff’s money.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE
9. The suit is valued at Rs. 20,00,000/- for the purposes of court fees and jurisdiction. Appropriate court fee as per the applicable court-fee law in force in {{STATE_NAME}} is payable thereon. Court fee affixed/payable: {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}}.

LIMITATION
10. The present suit is within limitation. The Plaintiff relies upon Article 47 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963 (For money paid upon an existing consideration which afterwards fails — three years — time begins to run from the date of failure). The suit is filed within three years from {{ACCRUAL_DATE}}, being the date of failure of consideration.

PRAYER
In view of the facts stated above, the Plaintiff most respectfully prays that this Hon’ble Court may be pleased to:

a. Pass a decree in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant for recovery of Rs. 20,00,000/- (Rupees Twenty Lakhs only);

b. Award interest on Rs. 20,00,000/- at such rate and for such period as this Hon’ble Court deems fit (including from {{ACCRUAL_DATE}} till realization);

c. Award costs of the suit; and

d. Pass such other and further orders as this Hon’ble Court may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.

VERIFICATION
I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, the Plaintiff above-named, do hereby verify under Order VI Rule 15 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908 that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 10 are true and correct to my knowledge and belief and nothing material has been concealed therefrom.

Verified at {{LOCATION}} on this {{FILING_DATE}}.


__________________________
Plaintiff

LIST OF DOCUMENTS / ANNEXURES
Annexure A: Proof of payment/receipt evidencing payment of Rs. 20,00,000/- as advance dated {{DATE_OF_PAYMENT}}.
Annexure B: Correspondence/communications exchanged between the parties in relation to the transaction and refund demands.

Place: {{PLACE}}
Date: {{FILING_DATE}}

blocking_issues.issue: Incorrect procedure invoked: draft is styled as a summary suit under Order XXXVII Rule 1 CPC without pleading the foundational requirements for Order 37 applicability (e.g., written contract/negotiable instrument, or other qualifying basis). Filing as an Order 37 plaint may be rejected/returned or lead to procedural defects.
blocking_issues.fix: Convert to an ordinary money recovery plaint (Section 26 CPC read with Order VII Rule 1 CPC) and remove Order XXXVII references from the title/body unless the plaintiff can specifically plead and support Order 37 eligibility.
blocking_issues.location: Title and opening line: “Plaint under Order XXXVII Rule 1…”

blocking_issues.issue: Mandatory jurisdiction pleadings are missing (territorial and pecuniary jurisdiction particulars under Order VII Rule 1 CPC).
blocking_issues.fix: Add a “Jurisdiction” paragraph stating (i) territorial jurisdiction facts (defendant resides/carries on business OR cause of action arose within the district) and (ii) that the suit valuation falls within the District Court’s pecuniary jurisdiction.
blocking_issues.location: After parties/introductory paragraphs (before Cause of Action)

blocking_issues.issue: Document reference label mismatch: List of Documents/Annexures contains Annexure A and Annexure B, but the body does not reference these labels at all.
blocking_issues.fix: Insert explicit references in the facts paragraph(s) to Annexure A (proof of payment) and Annexure B (correspondence/demand/communications), and keep the same labels in the List of Documents.
blocking_issues.location: Facts paragraph(s) and “LIST OF DOCUMENTS / ANNEXURES” section

non_blocking_issues.issue: Typographical/spacing error (“Pl aintiff”) and minor drafting inconsistencies in headings/capitalisation.
non_blocking_issues.fix: Correct typos and standardise spacing/capitalisation throughout.
non_blocking_issues.location: Paragraph 6 (“The Pl aintiff…”) and generally throughout

non_blocking_issues.issue: Interest prayer does not specify the claimed rate (left entirely to court discretion), which may be acceptable but is often clearer with a specific claimed rate and an alternative “such rate as the Court deems fit”.
non_blocking_issues.fix: Optionally specify a claimed rate (e.g., X% p.a.) with an alternative prayer for such rate as the Court deems fit; ensure consistency with Section 34 CPC practice.

final_artifacts.title: Plaint under Section 26 read with Order VII Rule 1 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908 for Recovery of Rs. 20,00,000/- (Advance Paid)
final_artifacts.text:
PLAINT UNDER SECTION 26 READ WITH ORDER VII RULE 1 OF THE CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE, 1908

IN THE COURT OF THE DISTRICT JUDGE AT {{DISTRICT_NAME}}, {{STATE_NAME}}

Civil Suit No. ____ of 20__

{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}},
S/o/D/o/W/o __________,
Aged about ____ years,
Residing at: {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}}                                             ...Plaintiff

VERSUS

{{DEFENDANT_NAME}},
S/o/D/o/W/o __________,
Aged about ____ years,
Residing at / Carrying on business at: {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}}                   ...Defendant

PLAINT

1. That the Plaintiff and the Defendant entered into a business transaction/arrangement under which the Plaintiff paid a sum of Rs. 20,00,000/- (Rupees Twenty Lakhs only) as an advance on {{DATE_OF_PAYMENT}} towards the said transaction.

2. That the Defendant committed default in performing its obligations in respect of the said transaction, as a result of which the transaction did not fructify and the consideration for which the advance was paid totally failed.

3. That the Plaintiff is entitled to refund/restoration of the said sum of Rs. 20,00,000/- on account of total failure of consideration, and the Plaintiff specifically pleads Section 65 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872.

4. That the Plaintiff has demanded refund of the said advance from the Defendant, but the Defendant has failed and neglected to refund the amount, and continues to wrongfully retain the Plaintiff’s money.

5. The Plaintiff relies upon the following documents:
   a. Proof of payment/receipt evidencing payment of Rs. 20,00,000/- as advance dated {{DATE_OF_PAYMENT}} (Annexure A).
   b. Correspondence/communications exchanged between the parties in relation to the transaction and refund demands (Annexure B).

JURISDICTION
6. This Hon’ble Court has territorial jurisdiction to entertain and try the present suit because {{TERRITORIAL_JURISDICTION_FACTS}}.

7. This Hon’ble Court has pecuniary jurisdiction as the value of the suit for the purposes of jurisdiction and court fees is Rs. 20,00,000/- (Rupees Twenty Lakhs only), which is within the pecuniary limits of this Hon’ble District Court.

CAUSE OF ACTION
8. The cause of action first arose on the date when the consideration failed and the Plaintiff’s right to claim restoration/refund accrued, i.e., on {{ACCRUAL_DATE}}. The cause of action further arose and continues to subsist on subsequent dates when the Defendant failed/refused to refund the amount despite demands, and as the Defendant continues to wrongfully retain the Plaintiff’s money.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE
9. The suit is valued at Rs. 20,00,000/- for the purposes of court fees and jurisdiction. Appropriate court fee as per the applicable court-fee law in force in {{STATE_NAME}} is payable thereon. Court fee affixed/payable: {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}}.

LIMITATION
10. The present suit is within limitation. The Plaintiff relies upon Article 47 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963 (For money paid upon an existing consideration which afterwards fails — three years — time begins to run from the date of failure). The suit is filed within three years from {{ACCRUAL_DATE}}, being the date of failure of consideration.

PRAYER
In view of the facts stated above, the Plaintiff most respectfully prays that this Hon’ble Court may be pleased to:

a. Pass a decree in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant for recovery of Rs. 20,00,000/- (Rupees Twenty Lakhs only);

b. Award interest on Rs. 20,00,000/- at such rate and for such period as this Hon’ble Court deems fit (including from {{ACCRUAL_DATE}} till realization);

c. Award costs of the suit; and

d. Pass such other and further orders as this Hon’ble Court may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.

VERIFICATION
I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, the Plaintiff above-named, do hereby verify under Order VI Rule 15 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908 that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 10 are true and correct to my knowledge and belief and nothing material has been concealed therefrom.

Verified at {{LOCATION}} on this {{FILING_DATE}}.


__________________________
Plaintiff

LIST OF DOCUMENTS / ANNEXURES
Annexure A: Proof of payment/receipt evidencing payment of Rs. 20,00,000/- as advance dated {{DATE_OF_PAYMENT}}.
Annexure B: Correspondence/communications exchanged between the parties in relation to the transaction and refund demands.

Place: {{PLACE}}
Date: {{FILING_DATE}}
